title: AI Provider Taxonomy
description: Schema for tracking AI model-provider products against stable capabilities. The capability is the anchor; products are 0..n per capability per provider.

_meta.description: Optional dataset-level metadata (description, as_of date, etc.).

capabilities.description: The stable taxonomy rows. These rarely change.

products.description: Concrete products that fulfill capabilities. These change constantly.

categories.description: Optional. Per-function information-architecture groupings of feature-axes (capabilities). Purely structural and provider-agnostic; groups + orders existing capabilities for one function's drill-down view. Absence means a function renders ungrouped (as before).

id.description: kebab-case stable key, scoped to its function, e.g. 'agentic-coding/agent-management'.

function_id.description: The capability id this category groups feature-axes within (the 'function').

feature_axis_ids.description: Capability ids (feature-axes) presented under this category for this function. May be empty when the category is derived from another field (e.g. 'Surfaces' from the surfaces enum).

tier.description: Which layer of the stack this capability sits in.

aliases.description: Other names people search for; helps an LLM map a fuzzy query to the right row.

name.description: Display name. Use '(none)' for a modeled absence.

kind.description: Granularity of this node. 'feature'=a capability inside a product (set parent_id); 'model_family'=a tiered group of models; 'service_tier'=a plan/tier. This is what lets the taxonomy hold everything from a model family down to a single feature.

parent_id.description: Optional. The node this one belongs to (feature -> product, model -> model_family, sub-product -> product). Enables hierarchy and the feature level.

review_status.description: Discovery-pipeline state. Newly discovered items land as 'candidate' or 'needs_review' (optionally under the 'unclassified' capability) until a human/agent confirms and assigns a capability. 'scaffold'=structural placeholder added by hand (e.g. a sub-feature), not yet ground-verified; the reproducible-build replay skips it until the maintenance loop grounds it. Defaults to 'confirmed' when omitted.

capability_ids.description: All capabilities this product touches. >1 entry signals a scope mismatch (the product spans rows).

primary_capability_id.description: The single capability this product is best classified under.

relation_within_capability.description: How the product's scope compares to the capability. 'direct'=clean fit; 'partial'=covers only a slice; 'broader'=does more than this capability; 'none'=absence record.

status.description: Lifecycle state. 'sunset'=being retired; 'merged'=folded into another product; 'absent'=provider deliberately has no offering.

scope_note.description: Free text capturing nuance the structured fields can't: scope mismatch, migration caveats, bundling quirks.

lifecycle.description: Dated change events. This is how the app shows history and detects what is in flux.

date.description: ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Use the date of the change OR the date a future change takes effect.

event.description: Short verb tag, e.g. launched, updated, renamed, deprecated, sunset, merged, discontinued, scope_expanded, consolidation_announced.

source_url.description: Where this specific event was confirmed (optional).

url.description: Primary reference for the record's current state.

last_verified.description: When a human/agent last confirmed this record. Drives staleness warnings.

confidence.description: How sure we are the record is current. Lower confidence = re-verify sooner.
